message:
fix(leaderboard): credit social clicks for mixed-case GitHub logins (fixes #1515) (#1516)

* fix: retry affiliate fetch so social column isn't empty on cold start

On first visit the leaderboard's social/affiliate column is often empty,
only filling in after a manual refresh or two. The affiliate data comes
from https://console.kubestellar.io/api/affiliate/clicks, which is served
by Netlify Functions and can cold-start past a 5-second budget. The
existing `.catch(() => {})` silently swallowed the AbortError and left
`affiliateData` as `{}` until the next full page load.

Two changes:
- Bump `AFFILIATE_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS` 5s → 15s so a cold start usually
  succeeds the first time.
- On failure, retry once after `AFFILIATE_RETRY_DELAY_MS` (2s). Both
  attempts share the same success handler. If the second also fails we
  keep the existing silent-fallback behavior (affiliate data is optional).

Cleanup of the retry timeout on unmount uses the standard useEffect
cleanup return.

Reported by @xonas1101 (kubestellar/console#8858) via the Console
feedback flow.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(leaderboard): track social link clicks by utm_term (fixes #1515)

The affiliate clicks API (console.kubestellar.io/api/affiliate/clicks)
returns click counts keyed by GitHub login, lowercased — GitHub logins
are case-insensitive, and the mergeEntry() helper in the Netlify
Function normalizes keys to lowercase before writing. But the
leaderboard's per-row lookup used entry.login directly, which preserves
GitHub's original casing (e.g. 'Abhishek-Punhani', 'Arpit529Srivastava',
'AAdIprog'). Result: affiliateData['Abhishek-Punhani'] was undefined
even though affiliateData['abhishek-punhani'] had 5 clicks, so the
social column showed 'No affiliate clicks yet' for every
mixed-case-login contributor.

Fix: normalize incoming API keys to lowercase on ingest (belt-and-
suspenders — the API already lowercases, but this guards against a
future change), and lowercase entry.login before the lookup in the
Social column render. Contributors with lowercase logins
(xonas1101, rishi-jat, etc.) were unaffected and continue to work.

Note: the reporter also mentioned LinkedIn Insights showing 10 link
interactions vs. GA4's 5 clicks. That gap is expected — LinkedIn
reports impressions + previews in that count, and LinkedIn can strip
UTM params on in-app previews, so some clicks reach the site without
the utm_term dimension and don't get attributed. GA4's attribution
window and processing lag (24-48h for session-scoped dimensions) also
contributes. The fix here ensures every attributable click gets
credited to the right contributor.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
